_comment: Entra ID MFA Enforcement Conditional Access Policy Template
_nist: IA-2 (Identification and Authentication), IA-2.1 (MFA for privileged), IA-2.2 (MFA for non-privileged)
_usage: Replace BREAK_GLASS_GROUP_ID before applying. Apply via Microsoft Graph API.
displayName: REQUIRE: MFA for All Users — All Apps
_state_comment: Start in report-only mode. Monitor 1-2 weeks. Move to enabled.

users._comment: Include all users. Exclude break-glass group to prevent self-lockout.
users._nist: AC-2 — account management requires break-glass accounts for emergency access

applications._comment: All cloud apps. Scope to specific apps if you need gradual rollout.

platforms._comment: All platforms — Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, Linux. MFA is platform-agnostic.

clientAppTypes: _comment: Modern auth clients only. Legacy auth (exchangeActiveSync, other) is blocked by separate policy.

locations._comment: All locations — no location exclusion. MFA required everywhere including trusted networks.
locations._rationale: Insider threats and VPN-compromised devices exist on trusted networks. MFA everywhere eliminates location as a trust boundary.

grantControls._comment: Require MFA. No alternative controls — not compliant device, not password change.
grantControls._nist: IA-2.1 and IA-2.2 — multi-factor authentication required for all access

sessionControls._comment: Enforce sign-in frequency and disable persistent browser sessions
sessionControls._nist: AC-12 — session termination after defined inactivity or time period

signInFrequency._comment: Require re-authentication every 8 hours. Balance security with usability.
signInFrequency._rationale: Sessions that persist indefinitely allow attackers to ride hijacked sessions long after the initial compromise. 8 hours = standard workday.

persistentBrowser._comment: Never allow persistent browser sessions. Prevents session tokens persisting across browser restarts.
persistentBrowser._rationale: Persistent sessions on shared or lost devices allow unauthorized access after the user has left the device.

_apply_commands.verify: az rest --method GET --url 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies' | python3 -c "import json,sys; [print(p['displayName'], p['state']) for p in json.load(sys.stdin)['value'] if 'mfa' in str(p.get('grantControls',{}))]"
